titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Richard Merkin Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday April 7, 2021 at 03:04

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.physdesc: (57 boxes) + 4 broadsides, 1 roll, 1 record album storage

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Boxes 40, 61 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. For further information consult the appropriate curator.
Box 60 (record album storage): Restricted fragile material. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: June 2010 acquisitions: purchased from Derringer Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, the Alfred Z. Baker, Jr. Fund, and the Danford N. Barney, Jr. Fund.
December 2010 acquisition: purchased from Appledore Books on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund.
September 2011 acquisition: purchased from Jeff Hirsch Books on the Donald Windham-Sandy Campbell Collection Endowment Fund.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three groupings: June 2010 Acquisitions; December 2010 Acquisition; and September 2011 Acquisition.

bioghist.head: Richard Merkin (1938-2009)
bioghist.p: Richard Merkin (1938-2009) was an American painter, illustrator, and arts educator. Born in Brooklyn, NY, Merkin received his BA in art from Syracuse University (1960), a Master's Degree in art from Michigan State University (1961), and a master's degree in Painting (MFA) from the Rhode Island School of Design (1963). He started teaching at RISD in 1963 and remained there for over forty years. His artwork was exhibited widely and his illustrations frequently appeared in Vanity Fair and the New Yorker .

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Richard Merkin Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Various acquisitions associated with the collection have not been merged and organized as a whole. Each acquisition is described separately in the contents list below and titled according to month and year of acquisition.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Richard Merkin collection, which spans 1823 to 2009, contains extensive correspondence and artwork. The collection also contains photographs, clippings, ephemera, audiovisual materials, objects, scrapbooks, and personal papers related to Merkin's life and work.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Richard Merkin Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. New York Correspondence School correspondence and papers, 1964 - 1973. II. Papers related to Richard Merkin and Virginia F. Fritz, 1911 - 1963.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains artwork, correspondence, personal papers, objects, and other materials by or related to Richard Merkin's life and work.

did.unittitle: New York Correspondence School correspondence and papers

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, artwork, photographs, printed material, and ephemera related to Ray Johnson, the New York Correspondence School, and Richard Merkin.

did.unittitle: Papers related to Richard Merkin and Virginia F. Fritz

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials within this series maintain the original physical organization found at time of acquisition with the exception of some oversized materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, photographs, artwork, printed material, and ephemera related to the lives and work of Virginia F. Fritz and Richard Merkin.

did.unittitle: Richard Merkin--drawings on napkins and photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Wishbones discarded. Preservation photocopy made.

did.unittitle: Shoebox lids sent from Virginia Fritz to Richard Merkin

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Collage contains a photograph of Richard Merkin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a photograph of Richard Merkin as a boy, dressed in a Tarzan costume.

did.unittitle: Framed artwork--"I love you, bunny, Easter '68"

did.physdesc: (49 boxes), 4 broadsides, 1 roll, 1 record album storage

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into nine series: I. Mail art, 1950 - 2009. II. Personal papers, 1907-05-12 - 1999. III. Correspondence, 1941 - 2009. IV. Erotic photographs and related materials, 1880 - 1999. V. Popular culture materials, circa 1880s-1998. VI. Scrapbooks and photograph albums, circa 1900s-2000s. VII. Sports related material, 1908 - 2002. VIII. Printed material, 1931-2000. IX. Audiovisual materials, 1940 - 1967.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, artwork, clippings, ephemera, scrapbooks, photographs, along with personal and business papers by or relating to Richard Merkin's life and work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains mail art sent to Richard Merkin, including postcards, flyers, and related correspondence.

did.unittitle: Mail art removed from correspondence files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Jann Haworth correspondence, along with letters which reference Lucian Freud, John Lennon, and R.B. Kitaj.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence to and from Seaver Leslie.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondent may also use the pseudonym "Darbus-Warbus".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes issues of the New Yorker, Vanity Fair, and GQ.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondent also goes by "Mary" and may also use the pseudonym "Pumpkin".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notebooks, photographs, printed material, clippings, artwork, and other personal papers by or related to Richard Merkin.

Yearbook and diploma from Erasmus Hall High School

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes eight "Art in America" covers by Todd McEwen.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes family photographs and artwork.

Scrapbook of newspaper clippings relate to New England artists

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Tin contains a green plastic gemstone and a red dog-shaped bead.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, postcards, and related materials sent to and from Richard Merkin. Notable correspondents include Maurice Sendak, Patti Smith, R. Crumb, and Ray Johnson, among others.

did.unittitle: Vonnegut, Kurt to William D. Huber
Press release for "Works by Ray Johnson"
Clippings related to Merkin, Ray Johnson, and Disney
Holiday greetings from Virginia Fritz and Richard Merkin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes material from Bill Plympton and Diana J. Bryan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letters and postcards to and from Richard Merkin, along with related materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 1961-1967 correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 1968-1972 correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 1972-1979 correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence from 1976-1978.

scopecontent.p: Includes 1979-1982 correspondence.

scopecontent.p: Includes Topps baseball cards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains erotic photographs, artwork, flyers, color slides, posters, and prints.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two slide viewers, one lighted and the other unlighted.

"Two men and one nude woman"--composite photograph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, photographs, artwork, and other materials related to popular culture.

Empty book box for "The Silent Butler Returns"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Dorothy Dalton and Fannie Brice.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Portraits are inscribed to "Madeline Fuller", who is also nicknamed "Sunny" or "The Sunshine Girl".

did.unittitle: Sketches of NYU students and faculty
Caricatures of Rita Hayworth and Katherine Hepburn

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains three booklets of caricatures including drawings of Mickey Rooney, Clark Gable, Boris Karloff, Stan Laurel and Oliver Hardy, Buster Keaton, Groucho Marx, Harpo Marx, Bela Lugosi, Peter Lorre, Bob Hope, Charles Laughton, Greta Garbo, Gary Cooper, Jimmy Durante, Marilyn Monroe, Marlene Dietrich, Edward G. Robinson, and Fred Astaire.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Painting shows a representation of Mary Louise "Texas" Guinan.

"B. Ward Beam - Free Acts for Fairs Parks and Celebrations" poster

Photograph of Zeppo Marx with Rose Janofsky and others
Photograph of Circus Parade in Seattle, Washington
Photograph of Marilyn Monroe on USO tour in occupied Japan
Photograph of Gayety Theatre in St. Louis, Missouri
Photograph of Richard Merkin sitting on bed
Photographic portrait of a young woman
"The Grand Old Chew Climax Plug" canvas advertisement sign
"10 inch Disappearing Gun in Action" photograph with woman's head superimposed.
Photograph of Greta Garbo 'Queen Christina' billboard at Astor Theatre, NY
"Here is where the Woolworth 5 and 10 cent stores had their beginning - Utica, New York"--photograph
"Underwood... The Machine of Champions"--canvas typewriter advertisement
"It May Seem Queer to You, But... They're Doing It For Their Health"--photographic transparency

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photograph albums, scrapbooks, and related materials collected by Richard Merkin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbook once belonged to Aldo Rubano.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Albums appear to be from different families. Notable images include photographs of silent-movie actress Jacqueline Logan and bandleader Mal Hallett.

did.unittitle: Photography album for the Orlando Canizares family

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, clippings, albums, typescripts, photographs, and artwork, much of which is related to baseball and other sports.

did.unittitle: Bound typescripts of "Footprints on the Sands of Time: Tales of Places and People in the Sporting Life of Old New York"
Baseball Hall of Fame lifetime pass and postcards
African-American and Cuban boxing photographs and negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Harry Wills, "Kid Chocolate" and "Black Bill".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photograph depicts players including Dizzie Dean, Paul Dean, and Leo Durocher. Teams represented include the House of David, the St. Louis Cardinals, the Boston Braves, and the High School Athletic Association, among others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes catalogs, invitations, flyers, posters, magazines, and other printed matter largely related to art exhibitions and events.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains items removed from printed matter in the collection.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains commercially-produced musical recordings and one film related to baseball.

"Gerry Mulligan Quartet (Pacific Jazz Records EP4-1)"--45 rpm record

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain the order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence from Tom Wolfe to Richard Merkin.
